## Highlights

`feishu-md-sync@0.6.3` is the recovery release for `feishu-docx-engine@0.1.1`. The immutable `v0.6.2` workflow correctly stopped before npm publication because its manifest had been generated with Node 23/npm 10 while the protected workflow packed with Node 24/npm 11. This release retains the unpublished engine version, advances the CLI patch, and makes artifact generation reproducible with one exact toolchain.

- Pin release packing and publication to Node 24.18.0 and npm 11.18.0.
- Record the exact artifact toolchain in the committed release manifest and reject runtime toolchain drift before packing or publication.
- Generate two independent engine and CLI packs and require byte identity before writing or accepting manifest hashes.
- Run a dedicated PR workflow whenever release manifests or their artifact tooling change.
- Retry only the readback phase after a successful Code create; never replay the provider mutation.
- Accept a later verified revision for image-to-Whiteboard replacement while rejecting lagging readback, unrelated drift, or mismatched raw content.
- Accept untampered schema-v1 prepared batches and recovery checkpoints produced by engine 0.1.0 under engine 0.1.1.
- Preserve tracked Supademos only when receipt, baseline placement, `isShowcase`, semantic locators, block identity, token, and remote shape remain exact.

## Installation

Install the CLI and matching Agent Skill from the same release:

```bash
npm install --global feishu-md-sync@0.6.3
npx skills add 'liyun95/feishu-md-sync#v0.6.3' --skill feishu-md-sync --global --yes
```

Library consumers can install the engine directly:

```bash
npm install --save-exact feishu-docx-engine@0.1.1
```

The v0.6 Skill requires `feishu-md-sync >=0.6.0 <0.7.0`.

## Artifact reproducibility

- Release manifests are generated and checked only with Node 24.18.0 and npm 11.18.0.
- The manifest records both toolchain versions plus npm integrity and local SHA-256 for both packages.
- Two independent packs must be byte-identical before `--write` updates the manifest.
- The Release PR runs the same `--check` command in GitHub Actions with the same exact toolchain.
- The tag workflow rechecks runtime toolchain identity and packed bytes before either npm publish step.

## Upgrade compatibility

- `feishu-docx-engine@0.1.1` emits new batches with engine version 0.1.1 and accepts valid schema-v1 batches/checkpoints from 0.1.0.
- Future engine versions, unsupported schemas, fingerprint mismatches, stale snapshots, and unrelated remote drift remain hard stops.
- `feishu-md-sync@0.6.3` pins engine 0.1.1 exactly.

## Safety model

- The failed v0.6.2 workflow published neither package and created no GitHub Release.
- No tag is moved or reused; v0.6.3 uses a new immutable release anchor.
- Code retry remains readback-only and never repeats a successful create.
- Whiteboard revision tolerance applies only after exact structural and raw-resource verification.
- No engine or CLI path automatically falls back to document replacement.

## Release ordering

The protected workflow publishes `feishu-docx-engine@0.1.1` first from `v0.6.3`, verifies byte identity and provenance, installs the CLI candidate with the registry-resolved engine, and only then publishes `feishu-md-sync@0.6.3`.

## Known boundaries

- Live write verification uses the dedicated controlled Feishu document and runs serially.
- Automatic merge remains available only for `gfm`; authoring dialects require independent pull and manual reconciliation.
- Feishu synced Code blocks remain manually provisioned and protected.
- Project B should update its exact engine pin only after 0.1.1 and 0.6.3 registry artifacts and provenance are verified.
